machine: granite-rapids (c8i.2xlarge, Xeon 6975P-C, 8 vCPU, 15GB RAM, EBS gp3 root nvme)
storage: EBS gp3 over nvme; measured cold sequential read = 135 MB/s (cat 4.096 GB in 30.24 s)
method: single-thread serial per-query latency. first_ms = coldest (nothing cached). cold = page cache dropped (drop_caches) before run; warm = re-run cached. c=200 rerank width, rotate x2.
note: ECONOMICS of disk-resident vectors. Exact (matrix) search touches all N/query -> from cold disk = 30.2 s/query (4GB/135MBps), confirming it MUST be in RAM. The binary funnel only scans 128MB codes + reads C=200 vectors/query, so the 4GB f32 can live on disk: hybrid (codes in RAM, f32 on disk) = 92 ms cold / 11.5 ms warm at 128MB resident RAM (32x less). Funnel disk cost is O(C) random reads INDEPENDENT of N; exact disk cost is O(N) the whole dataset. That is the scale-out unlock: shrink RAM ~32x, and if the working set fits page cache it is free (= full-RAM speed).

runs.note: full f32 scan in RAM; bandwidth-bound (~4GB read/query)
codes+f32 in RAM (in-memory winner); 60x faster than exact-RAM
codes in RAM, 200 f32 reads from COLD disk (~0.4ms/random read serial)
f32 now page-cached -> == full RAM, at 128MB committed
codes+f32 mmap, cached -> RAM speed at ~0 committed RAM
